SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

Accession: S24-01156
Date Received: 16/01/2024
Date Reported: 18/01/2024

Patient: Susan Mitchell  DOB: 19/02/1968
MRN: 442356
Clinician: Dr. Park, Dermatology
Specimen: Skin biopsy, left forearm
Clinical Diagnosis: Suspicious pigmented lesion

Gross Description:
Received in formalin is a single 0.8 x 0.5 x 0.3 cm skin ellipse
with a central brown-black macule measuring 0.4 cm. Inked margins.

Microscopic Description:
Sections show skin with a proliferation of atypical melanocytes
at the dermal-epidermal junction and in the superficial dermis.
The lesion demonstrates architectural atypia with irregular nests
and single cells at periphery. Cytologic atypia is present.
No ulceration identified.
Mitotic rate: 0/mm2
No lymphovascular invasion.
No regression.
Margins: Inked peripheral and deep margins are clear.
Breslow thickness: 0.5 mm
Clark level: III

Diagnosis:
Left forearm, skin biopsy:
- Malignant melanoma, invasive
  Breslow thickness: 0.5 mm
  Clark level: III
  No ulceration
  Mitotic rate: 0/mm2
  Margins clear

Dr. Helen Ross, MD
Board Certified Anatomic Pathology & Dermatopathology
